title: dryas octopetala var. tenella
intro: Dryas octopetala var. tenella, also known as the mountain avens, is a flowering plant species native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin issues, and infections. The plant has been used to promote overall health and well-being, with potential benefits including reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and enhanced immune function. Herbal preparations of Dryas octopetala var. tenella can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
